questions.question: What happens if one of the two GIA 63 units fails?

options.text: The autopilot operates in a degraded mode with reduced capability
The remaining GIA 63 takes over all autopilot functions
The autopilot cannot operate — both GIA 63 units are required
Only the yaw damper remains functional

questions.explanation: Both GIA 63 units are required for autopilot operation. GIA #1 specifically handles flight director computations. If either fails, the autopilot cannot function.

questions.question: What is the difference between pressing the FD button and the AP button?

options.text: FD engages the servos; AP shows guidance on the PFD
FD activates only the flight director for guidance; AP engages the servos to actually control the aircraft
They are interchangeable and perform the same function
FD controls lateral modes only; AP controls vertical modes only

questions.explanation: FD activates only the flight director, showing command bars on the PFD for hand-flying guidance. AP engages the autopilot servos to physically control the aircraft. You can have FD without AP, but AP always includes FD.

questions.question: Which autopilot mode maintains a selected airspeed during climbs and descents?

options.text: VS (Vertical Speed)
ALT (Altitude Hold)
FLC (Flight Level Change)
VNAV (Vertical Navigation)

questions.explanation: FLC (Flight Level Change) mode maintains a selected airspeed by adjusting pitch while climbing or descending to a new altitude.

questions.question: Below what altitude AGL is altitude capture prohibited with the autopilot?

options.text: 500 feet AGL
200 feet AGL
1,200 feet AGL
2,000 feet AGL

questions.explanation: Altitude capture below 1,200 feet AGL is prohibited because the autopilot's altitude capture mode could command abrupt pitch changes at low altitudes where recovery margins are small.

questions.question: Can the yaw damper operate independently of the autopilot?

options.text: No, the yaw damper requires the autopilot to be engaged
Yes, it can be used during hand-flying for a smoother ride
Only when the flight director is active
Only below 10,000 feet MSL

questions.explanation: The yaw damper can operate independently of the autopilot. It smooths out Dutch roll oscillations and can be used during hand-flying for a smoother ride.
